title: leucanthemum achilleae
intro: Leucanthemum achilleae, also known as Achillea millefolium, is a perennial flowering plant in the sunflower family native to Europe and Asia. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing due to its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing menstrual cramps, relieving anxiety and stress, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Leucanthemum achilleae is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, making it a popular choice among herbalists for its versatility and effectiveness.
